title: Ski-Resort Lift Spares: Two-Echelon Safety-Stock Sizing (METRIC)
strategies: decoupled equal-budget: warehouse near-perfect, every lift given the same expected-backorder budget cap/N (baseline/trivial)
per-lift marginal cost analysis: add spares where downtime-saved minus holding pays off, top up to the SLA cap by cheapest availability-per-dollar
two-echelon co-optimization: sweep the warehouse order-up-to level, recompute the METRIC lead-time coupling, and re-run the lift marginal allocation to exploit central risk-pooling
zero-inventory (infeasible): hold nothing, violating the service-level constraint -> rejected
